title: TIMEMORE Chestnut C2 Manual Coffee Grinder Capacity 25g with CNC Stainless Steel Conical Burr - Internal Adjustable Setting,Double Bearing Positioning,French Press Coffee for Hand Grinder Gift
description:
About this item
Metal body 2024 upgraded version,High quality 420 stainless steel burrs: The burrs are formed by cutting high-quality 420 stainless steel through a five axis CNC. The hardness of 55-58HRC can quickly cut coffee beans, and the dual bearing center axis positioning - grinding coffee powder particles evenly, so you can quickly grind a coffee powder that meets the taste.
Grinding degree adjustment function: built-in grinding degree adjustment knob, simply turn the grinding knob to adjust the manual coffee grinder to obtain the desired coffee taste (clockwise for fine grinding degree, counterclockwise for coarse grinding degree). You can enjoy a wonderful coffee time at home, in the office, or during outdoor camping and hiking, such as pour over coffee ,moka pot coffee , french press coffee,etc.
Easy to disassemble and clean: The compact design makes it easy to disassemble without the need for any tools. Do not rinse with water, use the complimentary brush to complete the cleaning. In general, it is not recommended to disassemble and replace parts of the bean grinder. This helps improve the accuracy of grinding, and if necessary, please follow the instructions in the manual.
Convenient to carry: You can obtain TIMEMORE C2 grinder X1, brush X1, cloth bag X1, and product manual X1; The weight of the bean grinder is 430g, while maintaining a lightweight and moderate sense of weight and luxury. It is not only suitable for home use, but also very suitable for carrying outdoors or traveling.
Service: If you have any questions or are not satisfied with the product, please feel free to contact us by sending us a message via Amazon. We will provide you with a response and a 100% satisfactory solution within 24 hours!
reviews_5s:
Let me begin by stating that I am most definitely not a coffee expert or purist. I do enjoy great coffee and espresso when I can. I depend on others to make the truly good stuff for me, and I can readily tolerate mediocre coffee as well.
I was impressed with the packaging and apparent quality of this grinder. It is packed with a cleaning brush and a sturdy fabric storage bag. The grinder seems to me to be well engineered and is heavily built.
It is very easy to use and quickly grinds beans to the ideal coarsenes for my morning single cup k-cup brews. I have not used it for other grinds yet, but it has an impressive range of adjustability of coarseness. I assume it will produce other grinds as well as it does my typical one. The grinder is fairly easy to clean.
I've not had this long, so I can't comment thoroughly on its longevity. The build quality seems to bode well for long-term functionality though.
Pros: Well built, wide range of grinds, easy to use.
Cons: Not much to note here, but its capacity might be insufficient for some. It holds enough beans to produce grounds for 1 to 1.5 cups of drip brew, so multiple refills would be needed to make a multi-cup pot of coffee.I had been reluctant to purchase a manual grinder, fearing that it would take a lot of time and effort to grind by hand, as I have often heard about manual grinders. I had bought a somewhat higher-end electric grinder but it just didn't give consistent grinds for my pour-over and had too many fine particles that clogged my filter. This grinder is awesome! The grind is consistent and perfect every time, and it takes very little effort or time to grind. I crank out a full 18g load in a few effortless seconds. It is easily adjustable as well. I also love that it's very quiet and I'm not waking up my family every time I grind my coffee! Quality of materials and workmanship seem really solid. Don't waste your money on anything else. Seriously the best manual grinder out there for the money, I think. The textured outside is easy to hold. If I had to complain about anything, it would be that you have to be very careful screwing on the base, as it's fairly easy to cross-thread it. As long as you don't force it, you're not going to do any damage by accident though.Play Video
I received it a few days ago and these are my highlights about it:

1- The product quality is great.
2- The burrs are built with detail and look like those of a electric grinder and also look like stainless steel.
3- The grind adjustment is very precise
4- The grounded coffee is evenly distributed, something rare on a manual grinder.
5- You can get espresso grind with the proper adjustment

Conclusion: imagine you have an electric grinder but only works with your hands force.

Note: The instructions mention you have to set the burrs to its cero position turning it clockwise. After that, you should turn itcounterclockwise until the desired ground size, but the instructions mention this second step as clockwise, something that can’t be done.
——-
Hace tiempo estaba buscando un molinillo de café manual que hiciera un buen trabajo, y lo he encontrado.

1-Excelente calidad en el material
2-Los molinillos están construidos con mucho detalle, muy bien hechos. Parecen hechos de acero inoxidable.
3-Los ajustes para el grosor de la molienda son bastante precisos.
4-El café molido mantiene homogeneidad en el tamaño. Esto es muy importante para obtener un buen café, especialmente si se trata de espresso.
5-Se puede obtener una molienda fina para espresso, algo que es raro en un molinillo manual.I really liked this hand grinder, my first, and it is very quick to grind to fine. The only problem is that my arthritis in my hands is always at its worse first thing in the morning. I had to return this because instead of hand grinding being fun it just hurt and was hard to justify. Good grinder. Solid, quick, and well made.

I decided I was being a baby about my hand pain and bought this a second time and I really like it. I have to be sure to grip it in a way to minimize the stress on my hands, but it's worth it to have a nice grinder. I grip high on the grinder and hold it close to my body, which works for me.

Almost perfect V60 pour over at 18 clicks in photo.It works nicely and is easy to clean. Grinds smoothly and easily and feels sturdy. Sometimes it gets squeaky, but a drop of vegetable oil will solve that. Its capacity is only about 22 grams, which is a little small for my liking, but that's my only complaint.It only does one thing, but it does that thing perfectly and with style. Very consistent grind quality. Much easier to adjust than the original model (although it also did a great job grinding). Feels solidly built and as if it will never wear out.Been using this as my daily grinder to grind just above espresso grinds. Did not take long to dial in my grinds for the brew I wanted while using my Origami. Easy to clean so far with the brush and working great. Looks pretty nice and feels great using.I was initially skeptical even with the great reviews. I have a very high quality espresso grinder from Lelit but I like pour over coffee in the morning. The idea of grinding by hand, however, was really off putting. I was especially concerned about the time it would take. This grinder is fantastic. The size of the grind is so even. There are hardly and fine or oversized particles. It takes maybe 20 seconds to grind a full grinder of coffee, which is the perfect amount for my pour over Kalita 185 sized filter holder. I’m done grinding before my fast kettle boils water. The finish on the grinder is also gorgeous. I highly recommend. I have no direct experience of 1Zpresso etc so I’m not sure what more one gets by spending extra on a hand grinder; the quality here is already exceptionally high.Quality build. Nice for a one or two cupper pour over coffee, but if you need a full 10 cup pot - you're going to be refilling and cranking!Glad I didn't buy an electric grinder. We take this w/ us car camping. Sharp, grinds well, easy to use: just unscrew the top, put in the beans, replace top and turn the handle. THAT'S IT! Glad I chose this one. Thank you, Timemore
reviews_1s:
I purchased the TIMEMORE Chestnut C2 hoping to use it with my espresso machine, but unfortunately, it doesn’t perform well for fine grinding. While the build quality is solid, and it works decently for coarser grinds like pour-over or French press, the experience with finer settings is a different story.

The grinder really struggles with espresso-level grinding. Adjusting to the smallest grind size takes a long time and requires a lot of effort. It feels like you’re endlessly turning the crank, and the output is minimal, making it frustrating and impractical for daily espresso use.

If you're into drip coffee or manual brewing methods, this might work for you, but for espresso enthusiasts, this is not the grinder you’re looking for. Save yourself the time and consider other options designed specifically for espresso grinding.I liked the product but was curious why aluminum and stainless steel could not be washed. I wrote the company and they sent me the exploded diagram of the grinder and all the parts it contains. You can clearly see it has several parts that are zinc plated. From what I have been told, and the research I did when I got this diagram, zinc coated metal shouldn't be around food. Not only that, it can leach around acidic materials, and coffee is acidic. So I sent it back because I don't care to have something that has such material in it.This product has the most asinine design decision implemented that I've ever seen. The bottom disc is a separate piece that you need to constantly turn to tighten, but it doesn't actually have a functional method that allows you to do so, therefore it'll constantly loosen and fall off mid grind. Why is that a separate piece to begin with? If it needed to be for whatever cost or manufacturing reason, why on earth wouldn't you implement a feature to tighten it or keep it tight?? Use your money on a product that's actually functional.Can't review the product because I received a used grinder. It feels sturdy. I was excited to try it but I'll have to return itThis did not work out of the box. I then got the royal run about through customer service - the manufacture told me it was not their problem and I had to deal with it through the third party retailer. The retailer told me it was a manufacturing problem and I had to deal with it through the manufacturer. It was like the twin Spiderman Meme with both parties pointing at each other.
End result - it's in the trash and I wasted my money. Unless you are after a reasonably cool looking paperweight, I would not recommend.This product was sent to me used, and still had the grounds of the previous user in it. This is highly unsanitary and disgusting. Unacceptable, if I could I would give it zero stars. At least have the decency to clean the product properly.I was so excited for this product. I received it, the packaging looked great, the product looked great, put it together and it didn't work, I tried to turn the handle and it wouldn't turn. It was locked up. Tried to contact the company via their contract page, didn't seem to be working. So I sent an email, just to see if there was a way I could fit and use the product...over a week later, no word at all...NOTHING. so no customer support at all. its too bad. Just going to have to return it to amazonThis grinder is very high quality, without a doubt. But at the finer grind settings it takes more than 5 minutes to grind 15g of coffee beans. It's not even giving a little bit of resistance. It feels like it is just grinding one bean at a time. I can't recommend this thing at all- especially if you enjoy light roastsThis is great for everything else than making espresso. It won't grind your coffee bean fine enough to make anything else than drip coffee. But do you need a manual coffee grinder for that?It does not grind anything near to espresso quality nor does it fit 25 g.
I guess it was designed to harvest some curious looks at the campground and start a conversation about you tattoos or expensive dreadlocks. .
You also have to keep shaking it to get the burrs grip the beans; still, a bunch will remain in the upper chamber so pack it accordingly or you gonna ruin your backpack.
